verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 encodes a type II single-pass transmembrane glycoprotein. The long isoform has an N-terminal TM anchor (uncleaved signal-anchor) that stably localizes it to the plasma membrane outer leaflet. UniProt P15291 and multiple published studies (PubMed:1714903) document that the long-form B4GALT1 is 'mainly localized at the plasma membrane' where it functions as a cell-surface recognition molecule in cell-cell and cell-matrix interactions. Surface localization is via the protein's own TM anchor, not secondary recruitment.
